status_scope: Public maturity and gap status for CivitasX's own OrgAnchor adoption. This is not a new root authority statement and not a trust badge.

level_matrix.name: Minimal Verifiable Identity
level_matrix.summary: Root authority, signed official endpoint statement, public verify page, and CLI verification are present.

level_matrix.name: Public Verification Presence
level_matrix.summary: The Cloudflare Pages verification route is live and reachable, but the project still uses a shared provider subdomain and some domain controls require manual review.

level_matrix.name: Mirrored and Archived Identity
level_matrix.summary: The parent-path package includes a signed lockfile, a real IPFS Pinata CID, a real Arweave Turbo publication, a new OpenTimestamps calendar proof, and an immutable history snapshot. The new Arweave files have been hash-verified through Turbo; arweave.net propagation is still pending. The prior carrier package remains verified through both gateways and retains a Bitcoin-confirmed OpenTimestamps proof.

level_matrix.name: Disaster Recovery and Auxiliary Names
level_matrix.summary: A public-artifact recovery rehearsal passed using Cloudflare public routes and the GitHub public archive tag. Durable auxiliary naming remains incomplete.

level_matrix.name: Governance and Migration Ready
level_matrix.summary: The current authority is a truthful 1-of-1 Temporary Founder Authority. Real threshold custody and signed migration require independent long-term custodians and are not complete.

live_package_gaps.summary: This refreshed carrier-ledger package includes a signed organchor.lock.json publication ledger.

The signed ledger records the 2026-07-15 parent-path package on IPFS Pinata and Arweave Turbo, plus a new OpenTimestamps calendar proof. The new proof is pending Bitcoin confirmation; the prior proof remains Bitcoin-confirmed.

A real IPFS Pinata CID is recorded for the parent-path verification package: bafybeie7gdli6pislictn6qobwdkhkazdtbvwsjvtn5ijatjedb3626l4u. This improves carrier redundancy but does not make IPFS or Pinata the identity root.

The parent-path verification package was uploaded through Arweave Turbo: 45 files, directory hash sha256:dcb05c435e4d0d30d6090cc747e7f924ff3c8928cc27889f49efdbc626ebd790. Key files are hash-verified through Turbo; arweave.net propagation remains pending. The prior package remains verified through both gateways.

A public-artifact recovery rehearsal passed using Cloudflare public routes and the GitHub public archive tag; it verified public route bytes, JSON parseability, manifest artifacts, and 4 public signatures.
The project currently uses civitasx.pages.dev. A dedicated canonical domain and hardened naming strategy remain future work.
The current root authority is 1-of-1. Multi-custodian authority should wait for real independent custodians and must not be simulated.
No authority migration has happened, so no migration chain is included in the current package.
The current parent claims are supported by first-party evidence and reproducible public records, but not by independent S2/S3/S4 evidence.

recommended_next_actions.action: Harden the canonical domain and durable naming strategy.
recommended_next_actions.reason: Cloudflare Pages is acceptable for the current baseline, but a long-horizon parent project should not depend only on a shared provider subdomain.

recommended_next_actions.action: Upgrade and verify the 2026-07-15 OpenTimestamps proof after Bitcoin confirmation, and optionally repeat verification against a local Bitcoin Core node.
recommended_next_actions.reason: The new parent-path proof currently has calendar attestations and is pending Bitcoin confirmation; local full-node verification remains a stronger independent check after upgrade.

recommended_next_actions.action: Prepare threshold custody only when real independent long-term custodians exist.
recommended_next_actions.reason: Multi-custodian authority should not be simulated or rushed before the social prerequisite exists.

non_claims: This status does not mean CivitasX is endorsed by OrgAnchor.
This status does not prove that CivitasX is correct.
This status does not prove decentralized governance.
This status does not make Cloudflare, GitHub, IPFS, Arweave, or any carrier the identity root.
This status does not grant authority to issue new official CivitasX statements without the current valid root authority or a later signed migration.
